questions.question:
Consider the following ANSI C program.

 #include < stdio.h >
int main()
{
    int i, j, count;
    count=0;
    i=0;
    for (j=-3; j < =3; j++)
    {
        if (( j > = 0) && (i++))
        count = count + j;
    }
    count = count +i;
    printf("%d", count);
    return 0;
}
Which one of the following options is correct?

(GATE CSE 2021 SET-1)
options:
B.

The program will compile successfully and output 10 when executed

C.

The program will compile successfully and output 8 when executed

D.

The program will compile successfully and output 13 when executed
questions.correct_answer:
B.

The program will compile successfully and output 10 when executed
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following C program: 

#include < stdio.h >
int main( )
{
    int i, j, k = 0;
    j = 2 * 3 / 4 + 2.0 / 5 + 8 / 5;
    k -=  --j;
    for(i = 0; i < 5; i++)
    {
         switch(i + k)
        {
          case 1:
          case 2: printf("\n%d", i+k);
          case 3: printf("\n%d", i+k);
          default: printf("\n%d", i+k);
        }
   }
return 0;
}
The number of times printf statement is executed is ________.

(GATE CSE 2015 SET-3)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the C program below.


#include < stdio.h >
int *A, stkTop;
int stkFunc(int opcode, int val)
{
    static int size=0, stkTop=0;
    switch (opcode) {
        case -1: size = val; break;
        case 0: if (stkTop < size) A[stkTop++] = val; break;
        default: if (stkTop) return A[--stkTop];
        }
       return -1;
}
int main()
{
    int B[20]; A = B; stkTop = -1;
    stkFunc (-1, 10);
    stkFunc ( 0, 5);
    stkFunc ( 0, 10);
    printf ("%d\n", stkFunc(1, 0) + stkFunc(1, 0));
}
The value printed by the above program is __________.

(GATE CSE 2015 SET-2)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
What will be the output of the following C program segment?

 char inChar = 'A' ;
switch ( inChar ) {
case 'A' : printf ("Choice A\ n") ;
case 'B' :
case 'C' : printf ("Choice B") ;
case 'D' :
case 'E' :
default : printf ( " No Choice" ) ; }


(GATE CSE 2012)
options:
D.

Program gives no output as it is erroneous

questions.question:
Given the programming constructs
I. assignment
II. for loops where the loop parameter cannot be changed within the loop
III. if-then-else
IV. forward go to
V. arbitrary go to
VI. non-recursive procedure call
VII. recursive procedure/function call
VIII. repeat loop,
which constructs will you not include in a programming language such that it should be possible to program the terminates (i.e., halting) function in the same programming language

(GATE CSE 1999)

questions.question:
The conditional expansion facility of macro processor is provided to

(GATE CSE 1997)
options:
A.

test a condition during the execution of the expanded program

B.

to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the execution of the expanded program

D.

to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the process of macro expansion
questions.correct_answer:
D.

to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the process of macro expansion

questions.question:
Assume that X and Y are non-zero positive integers. What does the following Pascal program segment do?

while X <> Y do
if  X > Y then
    X := X - Y
else
    Y := Y - X;
write(X);


(GATE CSE 1995)
options:
B.

Divides the larger number by the smaller number

questions.question:
An unrestricted use of the "goto" statement is harmful because

(GATE CSE 1994)
options:
A.

it makes it more difficult to verify programs

B.

it increases the running time of the programs

C.

it increases the memory required for the programs

D.

it results in the compiler generating longer machine code
questions.correct_answer:
A.

it makes it more difficult to verify programs
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
An unrestricted use of the "go to" statement is harmful because of which of the following reason (s):

(GATE CSE 1989)
options:
A.

It makes it more difficult to verify programs.

C.

It makes it more difficult to modify existing programs.

D.

It results in the compiler generating longer machine code.
questions.correct_answer:
A.

It makes it more difficult to verify programs.
